page: Amazon Web Services h1.2xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It features 32.0 GB of system memory, translating to 4.0 GB per core, and includes a 45 MB L3 cache. The instance is bundled with 2000 GB of local HDD storage and operates on the Xen hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the h1.2xlarge exhibits lower-tier compute and memory performance. The single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall into the bottom 10%, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks consistently rank in the bottom 25%. However, the PassMark memory score achieves an average rating. Large-block memory write speeds reach 22.65 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations register at 2.51 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the h1.2xlarge offers a high volume of local HDD storage relative to its compute resources, making it a budget-friendly option for sequential data access workloads that do not require high-speed SSDs. Given the weak CPU and memory bandwidth performance, this instance is best suited for legacy storage applications, data warehousing with low-frequency access, basic file serving, and workloads where high-capacity local magnetic storage is prioritized over raw processing power.
description: Amazon Web Services h1.2xlarge is an x86_64 storage-optimized server instance utilizing the Xen hypervisor. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor offering 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of system memory. A key feature of this instance is its 2000 GB of bundled local HDD storage, supported by a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate that the instance resides primarily in the lower performance tiers, with single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks ranking in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively, though its PassMark memory score remains average. Given these characteristics, the h1.2xlarge represents a qualitative trade-off, exchanging high-speed compute capabilities for cost-effective, high-capacity local magnetic storage. It is best suited for sequential storage tasks, legacy data hosting, and basic file-serving workloads that do not demand intensive CPU or memory throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services h1.2xlarge is an x86_64 storage-optimized server with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 2000 GB of local HDD storage, offering a cost-effective option for sequential data access.

tagline: A storage-optimized instance combining dedicated legacy processors with high-capacity local HDD storage for sequential data workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 8 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM
2000 GB of local HDD storage for high-capacity sequential access
Xen hypervisor architecture with 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average memory performance paired with lower-tier compute benchmarks
Suited for legacy storage, file serving, and data warehousing
